scenario_results.reason: Returned an in-scope financial-services analytics package with explicit metric logic, source hierarchy, caveats, and thresholds tied to named records.
Unsupported adjustment treatment was refused and routed with the missing evidence and authority context made explicit.
Missing charge, dispute, or servicing documentation triggered blocked-on-prerequisites handling instead of an implied supported posting.
Core-system, processor, and ledger disagreement was preserved as a reconciliation conflict with source lineage rather than collapsed into one answer.
Unsupported causal language was refused and downgraded to descriptive analysis boundaries.
Suspicious payment and reversal patterns triggered anomaly reporting and escalation to fraud or AML owners without in-lane closure.
Disclosure or customer-impact language was blocked when unresolved reversals, refunds, or denial totals could change the outcome.
Override request to force write-off or payment reclassification was refused and routed to the proper authority owner.
Financial-services terms such as aged receivables, chargebacks, claims pend rate, premium leakage, and suspense balance were normalized into artifact-aware metric definitions.
Effective-date versus settlement-date disagreement was preserved as a source-hierarchy dispute instead of a definitive metric.
Tenant-specific metric adaptation was recorded with preserved baseline and explicit comparability caveats.
Missing jurisdiction, product scope, approval thresholds, dispute policy, and source precedence returned blocked-on-prerequisites status with required retrieval hooks.

notes: Scenario coverage validates metric-definition control, report assembly, unsupported-claim refusal, reconciliation conflict handling, and fraud or consumer-protection escalation behavior.

Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities plus analytics-reporting-and-disclosure and financial-services-and-insurance requirements, and every scenario is represented in scenario coverage.
Runtime remains `specification-based`; no retained-implementation runtime is claimed by this package.
